Title: Mechanism of mixed-linkage glucan biosynthesis by barley cellulose synthase-like CslF6 (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan synthase

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT2 family cellulose synthase-like enzyme (CslF family); contains GT domain, plant-conserved region (PCR), class-specific region (CSR), 7 transmembrane helices, TED motif, QxxRW motif, switch motif
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: membrane-integral protein; Golgi/plasma membrane-associated transmembrane channel enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: mixed-linkage (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan biosynthesis / cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: grass cell wall mixed-linkage glucan branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into a linear mixed-linkage glucan containing predominantly (1,4)-β-linked glucosyl units interspersed with single (1,3)-β-linked glucosyl units
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell walls of cereal grains and grasses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber beneficial to human health; important determinant of cereal grain cell wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro assays required UDP-glucose and Mg2+; activity reduced with Mn2+ and absent with EDTA or Ca2+
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: purified recombinant protein in vitro enzyme assay with radiolabeled UDP-glucose; enzymatic digestion with lichenase/cellulase/(1,3)-β-glucanase; HPLC oligosaccharide profiling; glycosidic linkage analysis by PMAA GC-MS; cryo-EM structural analysis; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified barley HvCslF6 alone is sufficient to synthesize polymeric mixed-linkage (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan in vitro from UDP-glucose. 2. The product yields characteristic lichenase-generated DP3 and DP4 oligosaccharides and contains an approximately 1:2 ratio of (1,3)- to (1,4)-linked glucosyl residues. 3. HvCslF6 functions predominantly as a monomer and adopts a CesA-like fold with a transmembrane channel accommodating nascent glucan. 4. The enzyme directly determines formation of health-relevant cereal β-glucan dietary fiber.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant of mixed-linkage β-glucan formation; sufficient synthase for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable core target for engineering cereal grain dietary fiber content and structure; can be manipulated to alter total β-glucan and DP3:DP4 structural features, but linkage pattern control must be preserved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes biochemical sufficiency in vitro. Cryo-EM and pull-down data suggest HvCslF6 functions mainly as a monomer, unlike trimeric CesA enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT2 family CslF enzyme with switch-motif substitution
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: linkage-pattern control within mixed-linkage glucan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: mutation strongly shifts HvCslF6 product specificity away from mixed-linkage glucan toward largely cellulose-like (1,4)-β-glucan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms altered product instead of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber beneficial to human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; purified enzyme assay; hydrolase sensitivity profiling; glycosidic linkage analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Replacing Y787 with His markedly reduces (1,3)-β-linkage formation. 2. The mutant product is no longer appreciably degraded by lichenase or (1,3)-β-glucanase but remains cellulase-sensitive. 3. Linkage analysis shows the (1,3):(1,4) ratio drops from about 1:2 in wild type to about 1:24, indicating near-conversion to cellulose-like polymer synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly reduces mixed-linkage β-glucan character and shifts synthesis toward predominantly (1,4)-β-glucan/cellulose-like product
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: alters product identity away from desired dietary fiber structure
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: identifies Y787 as a critical residue to preserve or edit when tuning β-glucan fine structure; caution that mutation can collapse target mixed-linkage product into cellulose-like polymer
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutant enzyme retained stability/catalytic activity sufficiently for analysis, indicating the primary effect is on linkage specificity rather than gross protein folding.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT2 family CslF enzyme with CesA-derived switch motif replacement
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: linkage-pattern control within mixed-linkage glucan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: replacement of the entire CslF6 switch motif with the CesA counterpart largely abolishes (1,3)-β-linkage formation and yields mainly cellulose-like polymer
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms altered product instead of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber beneficial to human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: motif swapping; purified enzyme assay; hydrolase sensitivity profiling; glycosidic linkage analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Swapping the CslF6 switch motif with the corresponding CesA sequence greatly reduces (1,3)-β-linkage formation. 2. The product becomes lichenase-resistant and predominantly cellulase-sensitive. 3. Linkage analysis shows a drop in the (1,3):(1,4) ratio to about 1:32, demonstrating that the native switch motif is necessary for normal mixed-linkage glucan synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly suppresses mixed-linkage β-glucan formation and shifts product toward cellulose-like (1,4)-β-glucan
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of desired mixed-linkage glucan structure
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: supports engineering of switch-motif sequence to tune β-glucan structure, but major motif replacement is likely deleterious for desired product quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates the switch motif is necessary but not sufficient, because reciprocal introduction into CesA8 did not confer (1,3)-linkage synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT2 family CslF enzyme; GT-domain mutant
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: mutant enzyme still synthesizes mixed-linkage β-glucan but with slightly altered linkage ratio and reduced activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product with altered fine structure
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber beneficial to human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; purified enzyme activity assay; linkage analysis; oligosaccharide profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D629G slightly reduces (1,3)-β-linkage formation compared with wild type. 2. The mutant shows a DP3:DP4 ratio of about 1:2.5 instead of 1:2. 3. Overall catalytic activity is reduced, indicating a minor but measurable contribution of this residue to product structure and enzyme efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: slightly reduces (1,3)-linkage frequency and lowers total catalytic activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as principal control point; minor contributor
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: lower-priority target for fine-tuning β-glucan structural ratio, but weaker effect than switch motif and may reduce activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results section directly tests its causal effect on β-glucan fine structure.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT2 family CslF enzyme; TM-helix 4 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: mutant enzyme still synthesizes mixed-linkage β-glucan but with slightly altered linkage ratio and reduced activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product with altered fine structure
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber beneficial to human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; purified enzyme activity assay; linkage analysis; oligosaccharide profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. I757L slightly reduces (1,3)-β-linkage formation compared with wild type. 2. The mutant shows a DP3:DP4 ratio of about 1:2.5 instead of 1:2. 3. Overall catalytic activity is reduced, indicating a secondary role in controlling β-glucan fine structure.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: slightly reduces (1,3)-linkage frequency and lowers catalytic activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as principal control point; minor contributor
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible fine-structure tuning target, though weaker than switch motif and with activity penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results section directly tests its causal effect on β-glucan fine structure.
